title: Fixed Deposits: Can you save tax on FD interest? Key rules every depositor should know
summary: Interest earned on fixed deposits is treated as ‘Income from Other Sources’ under income tax rules. It gets added to your total annual income and taxed according to the income tax slab applicable to you.

title: Dearness Allowance: Uttar Pradesh announces 2% hike in DA for state government employees
summary: Dearness Allowance: Uttar Pradesh today announced a 2% hike in DA for state government employees, effectively increasing the component to 60% of basic pay from 58%.

title: How to build an emergency fund to stay financially secure amid layoffs, inflation and global job market uncertainty
summary: As job insecurity rises globally, building an emergency fund is essential for financial stability amid layoffs, inflation and AI-driven restructuring, ensuring protection through disciplined saving strategies.

title: Does your portfolio needs a global hedge in a ‘weaponised’ world?
summary: Geopolitical shifts are prompting Indian investors to reconsider strategies as economic equities become tools of statecraft. Experts emphasise the need for global diversification to mitigate local volatility and capture international growth amidst changing dynamics.

title: Secured credit cards: Unlocking credit and building a credit score for millions

title: Wholesale inflation hits a 42-month high — but does it really spell bad news for your wallet?
summary: India's wholesale inflation hit 8.3% in April, a 42-month high, mainly due to rising energy prices from the West Asia conflict. Experts note a weak correlation with consumer prices, suggesting retail inflation may not rise significantly despite the wholesale increase.

title: Gold turned ₹1 lakh into ₹15 lakhs in 20 years - Here's how equities, real estate and debt compare
summary: Equities in India and the US have outperformed fixed deposits, debt, and real estate over the long term. &nbsp;Mid-cap and small-cap stocks yielded the highest returns, emphasizing the importance of long-term investment.

title: ITC Q4 Results: Profit rises 5% YoY to Rs 5,113 crore; Rs 8/share dividend declared
summary: ITC reported a 5% year-on-year growth in its standalone net profit to Rs 5,113 crore for the March-ended quarter. Revenue from operations rose 17% YoY to Rs 21,695 crore, driven by a significant increase in excise duty on cigarettes. The company also recommended a final dividend of Rs 8 per share.
